data.body:
### [9/10] DOSSIER D — Coordination & storage posture: WHY + the one-time reclaim flag-day

EPHEMERAL-GHA TOPOLOGY (reconcile-bridge.yml): */20 cron, ubuntu-latest, timeout 60, fetch-depth 0, concurrency group reconcile-bridge cancel-in-progress=false. The reconciler is a TEMPLATE clients copy verbatim -> must run on stock hosted infra with NO persistent box; each pass = a fresh clone discarded at job end. The only "persistent" variant is the opt-in RECONCILE_CONTINUOUS flag (self-re-dispatch; warned self-hosted-only) — still chained-ephemeral, not a daemon. Ephemerality FORCES all durable state into git+origin (why .bridge_state is committed and the lock is a pushed ref) and forces fetch-depth 0 (shallow defeats merge-base/compaction, bug f193) + merge-not-rebase commit-back.

merge=ours DERIVED-CACHE POSTURE (init.py:56-63; sync.py): .bridge_state/* committed AND merge=ours (local-wins) because (a) off-git dies with the runner and the arbitration ancestor must survive; (b) merge=union is WRONG — it line-unions JSON into INVALID JSON; (c) `ours` because .bridge_state is the LONE non-disjoint shared path (all event files are UUID-named append-only-disjoint, never collide) and is a per-pass REGENERABLE cache the next pass rebuilds. push.py names prev_snapshot.json + bindings.json "reconciler-REGENERABLE files" (bugs 12a6/6818 stash/merge/pop). Rests on the 97e7/WU-2/3 UNION reconvergence (sync.py _do_reconverge/_union_merge): non-destructive, keeps both parents, stock git gc safe; conflict => abort+keep-local+hint-fsck, NEVER reset (the pre-97e7 reset --hard orphaned un-pushed local commits — the forcing data-loss incident).

COORDINATION SPLIT (ADR 0031 / epic 6c9c dust-troth-naval): pass-lock/phase-gate moved OFF-tree to bare refs/reconciler/lock + refs/reconciler/gate (create-only CAS acquire, observed-oid CAS release, skew-proof relative-duration lease + heartbeat/fence). "A lock is not ticket data." Children 524d/7522/2711/9ed2. WS-1 (event-sourcing the binding DATA off-branch) EXPLICITLY DROPPED — quote: "WS-1 dropped because Level-triggered bridge convergence (3006 + ADRs 0026-0029 + the 8de5 classifier/canary) already made binding drift detected and self-healing and bindings.json is a derived cache." The reconciler lock guards WASTED WORK (overlapping passes), never CORRUPTION — so a lost-race/stale .bridge_state copy self-corrects next pass and stays a committed derived cache; only the coordination state (needing cross-clone consensus) moved off-branch. Task 0241 (move .bridge_state off-branch) was SUPERSEDED by this drop.

gc-CORRUPTION (bug 88eb, "ADR 0051" — NOTE: not a committed file on this checkout; adr/ tops at 0050; named in the fix work): git>=2.47 `git commit` spawns a DETACHED `git maintenance run --auto` repack OUTSIDE rebar's lock -> races concurrent writers on the shared object DB -> corruption. gc.auto=0 does NOT fix it (git>=2.47 gates on maintenance.auto). Fix: FOREGROUND maintenance (gc.autoDetach=false + maintenance.autoDetach=false, unset gc.auto). The 97e7 reachability invariant answers "gc won't delete objects I need"; it is ORTHOGONAL to "a concurrent repack corrupts the pack while I write." A one-time filter-repo reclaim is SERIAL/quiesced so not exposed to the 88eb concurrency race (with auto-maint off).

TRUST/IDENTITY (authorship.py): ancestry-based — verify_authorship_at_commit builds the trust root from keyring records live as-of the event's INTRODUCING commit; key valid iff its add-commit is an ANCESTOR (git merge-base --is-ancestor) and revoke isn't; event->commit is RELATIVE (resolve_event_commit via git log --diff-filter=A). Everything is topology/ancestry, NOT literal SHA equality -> a CONSISTENT rewrite that preserves the DAG + keeps every event/keyring file STILL VERIFIES. THE ONE SHA-BOUND PIN: enforce_since="0cf8c035..." (rebar.toml:105, require_authenticated=true :96, now a PRE-merge whole-store Verified gate). _is_enforced (verify_authorship.py:263) does merge-base --is-ancestor <since> <commit> and FAILS CLOSED TO ENFORCED on any git error. A filter-repo rewrite destroys that literal SHA -> every event fail-closes to enforced -> the grandfather boundary collapses -> the whole-store gate fails. MUST re-pin enforce_since (and verify.opcert_enforce_since if set) via the filter-repo commit-map IN THE SAME PUSH.

ONE-TIME RECLAIM FLAG-DAY (8 steps): 1) disable */20 + RECONCILE_CONTINUOUS, drain in-flight pass, break-glass refs/reconciler/lock+gate. 2) FREEZE all writers. 3) filter-repo strip old .bridge_state blobs (NEVER touch event <hex>/<ts>-<uuid>-<TYPE>.json or keyring files), capture commit-map. 4) re-pin enforce_since (+opcert) to mapped SHA in the same push. 5) force-push rewritten tickets to origin. 6) re-clone/reset EVERY persistent auto-pushing clone to rewritten origin/tickets BEFORE it next writes — else sync.py:90-92 --allow-unrelated-histories UNION drags the stripped blobs back. VECTORS on this host: the operator-box main-checkout .tickets-tracker; every feature-worktree .tickets-tracker under .claude/worktrees/*; any running rebar-mcp process (restart it). NOTE the hourly origin/main tracker (CLAUDE.local.md) installs the TOOL BINARY from the code mirror — it does NOT clone/push tickets, so it is NOT a drag-back vector (correcting an earlier framing); the thing to quiesce is the running MCP/tool PROCESS. The GHA runner re-clones fresh so is safe IF the reconciler stays PAUSED until origin is rewritten. 7) re-init each tracker (applies foreground-maintenance gc config; git>=2.47 ignores gc.auto). 8) run `rebar verify-identity --all` -> 0 enforced-not-verified against the new pin; then resume the reconciler.

BEARING: (a) content-narrowing and (b) the debug artifact PRESERVE the storage posture (still regenerable caches, single JSON blobs, merge=ours still right); narrowing actually strengthens it by shrinking the only non-disjoint churning path. (c) is safe on the trust-ANCESTRY axis but has two hard coordination failure modes: the enforce_since re-pin (else the pre-merge gate bricks the store) and the --allow-unrelated-histories drag-back (else any un-converged clone resurrects the blobs).
